frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment:
A: Frankly, these days marriage has become a financial risk for men, yaar. One divorce and suddenly alimony, maintenance, 498A threat — the entire system assumes the man is guilty.
B: Hm.
A: I keep telling younger officers, be very careful. Aajkal many women look for a secure match first and if things turn sour, they know the law is entirely on their side.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially motivated and frames marriage/divorce laws as tools women exploit against men. It encodes victimhood inversion, legal framing around 498A misuse, and suspicion that women seek financially secure men for gain.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
